description.task: Implement a CodeSmellDetector class that analyzes simplified method signatures and class metrics to identify common code smells: Long Method (too many lines), Large Class (too many methods), and Feature Envy (method accesses more other-class fields than its own class).
instructions: detectLongMethods() is already implemented — do not change it
detectLargeClasses() filters classes where methodCount > methodThreshold and returns their names
detectFeatureEnvy() counts accessedClasses entries equal to ownerClass (own access) vs different (other access)
A method has Feature Envy when other-class access count > own-class access count
detectFeatureEnvy() returns the names of methods that exhibit Feature Envy
description.hint: For detectFeatureEnvy(): for each method, long ownCount = method.accessedClasses().stream().filter(c -> c.equals(method.ownerClass())).count(); long otherCount = method.accessedClasses().size() - ownCount; if (otherCount > ownCount) add to result.
description.expectedOutput:
Long methods (>50): [processOrder, sendEmail]
Large classes (>10): [GodClass]
Feature envy: [processOrder, sendEmail]

description.task: Create a SolidPrinciplesChecker class that evaluates class designs against SOLID principle metrics. Check Single Responsibility (one primary concern), Open-Closed (extension via interface), Dependency Inversion (depends on abstractions), and report compliance scores.
instructions: check() is already implemented — do not change it
checkAll() maps each ClassDesign to a ComplianceReport using check()
averageScore() calls checkAll() and computes the average of all report scores
averageScore() returns 0.0 when the input list is empty
Both methods should work correctly for any list size

description.task: Build a TechnicalDebtTracker class that models and quantifies technical debt items in a codebase. Each debt item has a type (CODE_SMELL, MISSING_TEST, OUTDATED_DEPENDENCY, SECURITY_VULNERABILITY), effort to fix (in hours), and priority. Calculate total debt hours and prioritize what to fix first.
instructions: totalDebtHours() sums all effortHours — already implemented, do not change
prioritized() sorts by priority descending (CRITICAL=3, HIGH=2, MEDIUM=1, LOW=0) then by effortHours ascending
debtByType() groups items by DebtType and sums effortHours per group using Collectors.groupingBy and summingDouble
quickWins() filters items where priority is CRITICAL or HIGH and effortHours <= maxHours, then sorts by priority desc and effort asc
Priority enum ordinal order: LOW=0, MEDIUM=1, HIGH=2, CRITICAL=3 — higher ordinal = higher priority
description.hint: prioritized(): Comparator.comparing(DebtItem::priority).reversed().thenComparingDouble(DebtItem::effortHours). quickWins(): filter Set.of(Priority.CRITICAL, Priority.HIGH).contains(priority) && effortHours <= maxHours, then apply same comparator.
description.expectedOutput:
Total debt: 31.0h
Top item: D1
Quick wins (<= 5h): [D1, D4]
